title: Siyaani Commerce - Ethnic wear, self-driving ops
subtitle: A 50-day store cycle run by a GRPO-tuned Qwen agent on the OpenEnv JSON action contract (saree, kurta, dupatta, cotton sets).

chapters.title: Chapter 1 - Storefront and stockroom
chapters.alt: Indian ethnic-wear display and store lighting at opening hour.
chapters.story: Siyaani is a D2C label built on sarees, silk kurtas, cotton coordinates, and dupattas. Every morning the founder juggles restocks, refunds, ad spend, and supplier follow-ups. That loop is now a Qwen2.5-1.5B policy, fine-tuned with GRPO, issuing strict JSON to the same OpenEnv server the judges use.

chapters.title: Chapter 2 - Craft and inventory
chapters.alt: Folded ethnic fabrics, embroidery, and colour stories for the Siyaani catalog.
chapters.story: SKUs in config map to the live observation: units on hand, your ticket queue, and competitor pricing per style. The agent must return exactly one of six action schemas. The engine validates; malformed JSON is rejected, not silently repaired.

chapters.title: Chapter 3 - Policy on the sales floor
chapters.alt: Busy retail floor with ethnic and fusion wear for everyday and festive use.
chapters.story: GRPO compares bundled samples on composite reward, not hand labels. The policy shifts from a refund-heavy floor toward restock, set_price, and negotiate. The proof tab’s entropy curve is the signature of that shift.

chapters.title: Chapter 4 - Beyond the training catalog
chapters.alt: Styling and display—same adapter, new business_id generalisation test.
chapters.story: The same adapter is evaluated on unseen configs, e.g. medplus_pharmacy and stackbase_saas, with no per-business prompt swap. The Generalisation tab shows per-config banks and grader lines—this is the stress test, not a slide deck claim.

transition.notes: Text uses .r2-fade-in-text; no parallax, no large hover scale (plan 8.3).
